answer: The Florida purchase escrow disbursement reconciliation overstatement for FNF National Title closings on September 15, 2026 was caused by the settlement_disbursement_recon_v3 pipeline incorrectly joining party_identity_bridge on normalized_tax_id instead of closing_party_role_id. The bad join key matched multiple buyer and seller party roles within the same closing transaction, duplicating escrow disbursement records in the finance.disbursement_recon_daily view. Remediation requires switching to the correct join key closing_party_role_id, quarantining normalized_tax_id joins in reconciliation workloads, and backfilling affected Florida purchase closings.

facts.affected_segment: Florida purchase closings for FNF National Title
facts.remediation: Switch the transform to closing_party_role_id, quarantine normalized_tax_id joins in reconciliation workloads, and backfill Florida purchase closings for FNF National Title
facts.root_cause: settlement_disbursement_recon_v3 joining party_identity_bridge by normalized_tax_id matched multiple buyer and seller party roles in the same closing, duplicating escrow disbursements
